body.title: [sprint] BUG (pre-existing): context-load run lacks violation_summary() its test expects
body.intention: I intend that the context-load violation-summary feature and its test agree — implemented or removed.
body.description:
**Problem (pre-existing — NOT introduced by the fs-store fix):** tests/test_context_load_violation_summary.py expects `run.violation_summary(n, days)`, but HEAD's skills/context-load/run has no such function (4 tests red at HEAD, confirmed via git show + stash). The old SKILL.md Step 5.9 referenced `python run violation-summary` — a subcommand the run script never implemented (run takes no args). The context-load SKILL.md rewrite (T-context-load-fs-store) dropped Step 5.9 to match the executor. **Shape:** decide + do — either implement violation_summary in the run flow (restore session-start rule-drift surfacing; read the skill-violation telemetry log; wire into main(); re-add SKILL.md Step 5.9), OR delete the abandoned test. **Affected files:** skills/context-load/run, tests/test_context_load_violation_summary.py, skills/context-load/SKILL.md. **Design rules:** none. **Scope boundary:** IN — reconcile feature vs test. OUT — other context-load work. **Test plan:** if implemented, the existing test passes; if removed, the test is gone and the suite is green. **Completion criteria:** tests/test_context_load_violation_summary.py either passes or no longer exists; SKILL.md matches the executor.

[error] skeleton/ owns the MCP aggregator and flat-file registry. No Postgres dependency. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] devices/ contains one subdirectory per device; each is independently deployable. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] datacenter_logs/<device>/<subsystem>/ is the log hierarchy. Never write to a flat — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] Every ticket closes proven, or declares itself unproven — no discriminator. A ticket that — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] PATTERN-001: BaseDevice / BaseShim Lifecycle: `rollback()` must be idempotent — safe to call even if — applies: tags: pattern, pattern-001 (source: docs/design_patterns_inventory.md#PATTERN-001: BaseDevice / BaseShim Lifecycle)
